ItacAcademyItem.Name: Imperfetti (Accademia degli)
ItacAcademyItem.Notes: It was probably created by one dottor Angiolucci in the Studio della Sapienza. Cesare Colonna`s poem in Degli Allori d`Eurota (1662), suggests that the Academy existed before 1662. Maylender found a different `impresa` for this Academy, representing a compass and a circle, with the motto `Fiet` ---- Verucci`s comedy, Il dispettoso marito, suggests that the Academy existed as early as 1612. --- Another publication related to this and other Roman Academies (not in British Library collections) is: Ercole Brasavola, Inquisitiones variae eruditionis in Intricatorum, Imperfectorum, et Indispositorum Academiis (1674). See Franchi, Impressioni sceniche, p. 561n
ItacAcademyItem.Citation: Lorenza Gianfrancesco and Simone Testa, directed by Jane Everson, Denis Reidy, Lisa Sampson, assisted by Thomas Denman, for the Italian Academies Database project.
